core/paragraph.title: Paragraph
core/paragraph.description: Basic text block for body content.
core/paragraph.hint: Use for any body text, descriptions, or prose. The default block for written content.

content.description: The paragraph text. Supports inline HTML.

dropCap.description: Renders the first letter as a large drop cap.

placeholder.description: Placeholder text shown when block is empty.

example.content: This is a sample paragraph with some text content.

core/heading.title: Heading
core/heading.description: Introduces content with a hierarchical heading (H1–H6).
core/heading.hint: Use for section titles and hierarchical headings. Default level is H2. Use H1 for page titles, H2–H3 for sections, H4–H6 for sub-sections.

content.description: The heading text. Supports inline HTML.

placeholder.description: Placeholder text shown when block is empty.

core/image.title: Image
core/image.description: Inserts an image with optional caption, alt text, and link.
core/image.hint: Use for single images. For multiple images in a grid, use core/gallery instead. Set alt text for accessibility.

example.caption: An optional caption for the image.

core/gallery.title: Gallery
core/gallery.description: Displays multiple images in a responsive grid layout.
core/gallery.hint: Use when the user wants to show multiple images together in a grid. Each image inside should be a core/image block.

columns.description: Number of columns in the gallery grid (1–8).

imageCrop.description: Whether to crop images to the same height.

core/list.title: List
core/list.description: An ordered or unordered list of items.
core/list.hint: Use for bullet points (unordered) or numbered lists (ordered). Each list item must be a core/list-item block.

ordered.description: True for a numbered list (ol), false for bullet list (ul).

start.description: The starting number for ordered lists.

reversed.description: Whether an ordered list counts down instead of up.

core/list-item.title: List Item
core/list-item.description: A single item within a core/list block.
core/list-item.hint: Always nested inside a core/list block. Each core/list-item represents one bullet or numbered entry.

content.description: The text content of the list item. Supports inline HTML.

core/quote.title: Quote
core/quote.description: A stylized blockquote with optional citation.
core/quote.hint: Use for short quotations that flow with content. For large standalone pull quotes, use core/pullquote instead.

value.description: The quoted text content (supports nested paragraph blocks).

citation.description: The source or author of the quote.

example.value: The best way to predict the future is to invent it.

core/pullquote.title: Pullquote
core/pullquote.description: A large, visually prominent quote displayed as a standalone design element.
core/pullquote.hint: Use for a single important quote you want to visually highlight. Styled more prominently than core/quote. Good for testimonials or key statements.

citation.description: The source or author of the quote.

example.value: Design is not just what it looks like — it's how it works.

core/table.title: Table
core/table.description: A structured data table with rows and columns.
core/table.hint: Use for tabular data with clear row/column relationships. Not for layout — use core/columns for layout grids.

hasFixedLayout.description: Whether columns have equal fixed widths.

caption.description: An optional caption displayed below the table.

head.description: Array of header row objects with cells.

body.description: Array of body row objects with cells.

foot.description: Array of footer row objects with cells.

core/code.title: Code
core/code.description: Displays a block of preformatted code with a monospace font.
core/code.hint: Use for code samples. Content is rendered as-is inside a <pre><code> element. For non-code preformatted text, use core/preformatted.

content.description: The code content. Whitespace and newlines are preserved.

core/preformatted.title: Preformatted
core/preformatted.description: Displays text exactly as typed, preserving spaces and line breaks.
core/preformatted.hint: Use for preformatted text where whitespace matters but it isn't code. For code, use core/code instead.

content.description: The preformatted text. Whitespace and line breaks are preserved.

example.content:
Name:    Alice
Role:    Developer
City:    Berlin

core/verse.title: Verse
core/verse.description: A block for poetry, lyrics, or other content where line breaks are meaningful.
core/verse.hint: Use for poems, song lyrics, or artistic text where every line break is intentional.

content.description: The verse text. Line breaks are preserved.

example.content:
Shall I compare thee to a summer's day?
Thou art more lovely and more temperate.

core/html.title: Custom HTML
core/html.description: Allows inserting arbitrary HTML markup directly into the page.
core/html.hint: Use only when no other block can achieve the desired output. HTML is output verbatim. Avoid for content that can be expressed with standard blocks.

content.description: Raw HTML content to output verbatim.

core/separator.title: Separator
core/separator.description: A horizontal rule to visually divide sections of content.
core/separator.hint: Use to create a visual break between sections. Supports dot, wide, and short style variants.

core/spacer.title: Spacer
core/spacer.hint: Use to add vertical space between sections. Set height in pixels. Prefer CSS spacing when possible, but this block is useful for quick visual adjustments.

height.description: The height of the spacer. Accepts pixel values (e.g. '100px') or other CSS units.

width.description: Optional width of the spacer element.

core/buttons.title: Buttons
core/buttons.description: A container block that holds one or more core/button blocks side by side.
core/buttons.hint: Always wrap core/button blocks inside a core/buttons container. Use for calls to action, form submissions, or link groups.

layout.description: Layout configuration — e.g. flex direction and justification.

core/button.title: Button
core/button.description: A styled clickable button with a URL link.
core/button.hint: Must be placed inside a core/buttons block. Supports primary (filled) and secondary (outline) styles.

url.description: The URL the button links to.

linkTarget.description: Whether the link opens in the same tab or a new tab.

width.description: Optional percentage width of the button.

core/group.title: Group
core/group.description: A container block that groups other blocks together, optionally with a background color, padding, or border.
core/group.hint: Use as a general-purpose container to group blocks and apply shared styling. Supports row and stack layout modes. Common for card layouts, sections, and CTAs.

tagName.description: The HTML element used for the group wrapper.

layout.description: Layout configuration — e.g. constrained, flex, or grid.

core/columns.title: Columns
core/columns.description: A multi-column layout block that holds core/column blocks.
core/columns.hint: Use to create multi-column layouts. Always contains core/column children. Each core/column can have a specified width percentage.

columns.description: Number of columns (auto-derived from inner core/column blocks).

isStackedOnMobile.description: Whether columns stack vertically on mobile screens.

core/column.title: Column
core/column.description: A single column within a core/columns block.
core/column.hint: Must always be a direct child of core/columns. Set width as a percentage (e.g. '33.33%', '50%'). Can contain any other blocks.

width.description: Column width as a percentage (e.g. '33.33%') or pixel value.

allowedBlocks.description: Optional list of block names allowed inside this column.

core/media-text.title: Media & Text
core/media-text.description: Displays an image or video alongside a block of text content in a side-by-side layout.
core/media-text.hint: Use for alternating image-and-text feature sections. The media appears on one side and the text content on the other. Supports flipping the media position.

mediaId.description: WordPress attachment ID of the media.

mediaUrl.description: URL of the media (image or video).

mediaPosition.description: Which side the media appears on.

mediaWidth.description: Width of the media column as a percentage (default 50).

isStackedOnMobile.description: Whether the layout stacks vertically on mobile.

imageFill.description: Whether the image fills its container area.

core/cover.title: Cover
core/cover.description: Displays an image or video as a full-width background with overlaid text or blocks.
core/cover.hint: Use for hero sections, banners, and full-width image backgrounds with overlaid content. Can contain heading, paragraph, and button blocks as inner content.

url.description: URL of the background image or video.

alt.description: Alt text for the background image (accessibility).

id.description: WordPress attachment ID of the background media.

overlayColor.description: Theme color slug for the overlay.

customOverlayColor.description: Custom hex color for the overlay.

contentPosition.description: Position of inner content (e.g. 'center center', 'top left').

hasParallax.description: Whether the background scrolls with a parallax effect.

core/video.title: Video
core/video.description: Embeds a self-hosted video file with player controls.
core/video.hint: Use for self-hosted video files uploaded to the WordPress media library. For YouTube, Vimeo, or other external video services, use core/embed instead.

controls.description: Whether player controls are visible (default true).

playsInline.description: Whether the video plays inline on mobile.

poster.description: URL of the poster image shown before play.

core/audio.title: Audio
core/audio.description: Embeds a self-hosted audio file with a browser-native audio player.
core/audio.hint: Use for self-hosted audio files (MP3, OGG, WAV). Renders as an HTML5 audio player. Not for external streaming services.

caption.description: Caption displayed below the audio player.

autoplay.description: Whether audio plays automatically on load.

loop.description: Whether audio loops when it ends.

core/file.title: File
core/file.description: Displays a downloadable file attachment with a button link.
core/file.hint: Use when you want visitors to download a file. Shows the filename and a download button. Supports PDFs, Word documents, and any other file type.

id.description: WordPress attachment ID of the file.

textLinkHref.description: URL for the text link portion.

textLinkTarget.description: Whether the file opens in a new tab.

showDownloadButton.description: Whether to display the download button.

displayPreview.description: Whether to show a PDF preview inline (PDF files only).

previewHeight.description: Height in pixels of the PDF preview.

core/embed.title: Embed
core/embed.description: Embeds content from external services such as YouTube, Vimeo, Twitter, Spotify, and hundreds of oEmbed-compatible providers.
core/embed.hint: Use for embedding external content by URL. Paste a URL from a supported service (YouTube, Vimeo, Spotify, Twitter/X, Instagram, TikTok, etc.) and WordPress will automatically fetch an oEmbed preview.

url.description: The URL of the content to embed.

caption.description: Optional caption displayed below the embed.

core/search.title: Search
core/search.description: Displays a site search form with a text input and submit button.
core/search.hint: Use to add a site search form to a page or template. Supports customizing the label, placeholder, and button text.

label.description: Visible label for the search input (set showLabel to false to hide it).

placeholder.description: Placeholder text inside the search input.

buttonPosition.description: Position of the submit button relative to the input.

buttonUseIcon.description: Whether the button displays an icon instead of text.
